search_queries_used: site:doh.gov.ae antenatal care standard routine pregnancy follow-up Abu Dhabi
candidate_sources_rejected: DHA Standards for Obstetric and Neonatal Services
rejection_reasons: The DHA document is primarily a facility-service standard and was not as exact for visit content as the DoH antenatal standard.
The EHS page is a service announcement rather than a detailed clinical standard.
NICE NG201 was reviewed but the current UAE DoH standard was selected as the primary exact source.
population_applicability: Direct for routine antenatal care, but high-risk and complication-specific management is outside the mapped baseline scope.
setting_applicability: Partial: source applies to DoH-licensed Abu Dhabi antenatal services; the baseline is setting-agnostic.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DoH-licensed antenatal providers in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates; broader UAE application is not asserted.
recency_verification: Official V1 standard published July 2024, effective June 2024, opened on 2026-07-11, and due for revision July 2027.
superseded_check: No newer official DoH antenatal-care standard was identified on 2026-07-11.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports routine antenatal identity, gestational context, history, risk reassessment, counselling, patient concerns, care-plan, record, follow-up, and clinician referral documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines routine antenatal documentation, risk review at every visit, history and record review, patient concerns, shared decisions, counselling, care plans, and high-risk referral boundaries.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped weight, blood pressure, urine dipstick, fetal movement, fetal heart, fundal height, laboratory, ultrasound, OGTT, counselling, and gestation-linked follow-up fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies gestation-linked weight, blood pressure, urine dipstick, fetal heart, fundal height, fetal movement, laboratory, ultrasound, glucose-screening, counselling, and follow-up documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports escalation only as clinician-assessed high-risk referral documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines conditions that require additional obstetric review and preserves escalation as a clinician decision rather than an automatic workflow output.

unresolved_source_gaps: The baseline includes symptom and red-flag fields not all stated in the reviewed routine sections.
High-risk and complication-specific management remains outside this mapping.
Medication or supplement decisions and fixed clinical interpretations remain unsupported.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
